name: Self-interacting dark matter (Yukawa-mediator)
summary: Dark matter has sizable self-scattering through a light mediator (dark photon or dark Higgs), with cross-section sigma/m ~ 1 cm^2/g at dwarf-galaxy velocities decreasing to ~0.1 cm^2/g at cluster scales. The velocity-dependent cross-section comes from a Yukawa potential: Sommerfeld enhancement at low v, Born-regime suppression at high v. Resolves galactic small-scale-structure problems (core-cusp, missing satellites, too-big-to-fail) while respecting cluster-merger bounds.

addresses_puzzles.note: sigma/m ~ 0.5 - 5 cm^2/g at dwarf-galaxy velocities (10-100 km/s) thermalises DM in halo centres and produces flat-cored profiles. Sigma/m falls at cluster velocities (>1000 km/s) due to the Yukawa-mediator velocity dependence, satisfying Bullet Cluster bounds (sigma/m < 0.47 cm^2/g at v ~ 4000 km/s).
Specifies the SIDM particle and its mediator; thermal-relic freeze-out fixes the abundance for a given mediator coupling. Connects DM physics to dark-photon / dark-Higgs hidden-sector phenomenology.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicts sigma/m ~ 0.1-0.5 cm^2/g at cluster velocities, just below the Harvey-Massey-Kitching cluster-merger bound 0.47 cm^2/g. Velocity dependence is the key distinguishing prediction relative to constant-cross-section SIDM.
Velocity-suppressed cross-section at high relative velocities (v_rel ~ 4000 km/s in Bullet Cluster) saturates but doesn't violate the merger-offset bound.
If the mediator is a dark photon with SM kinetic mixing, predicts direct-detection and beam-dump signals correlated with the SIDM parameter region.

new_fields: light mediator (dark photon or dark Higgs)
introduces.note: DM mass, mediator mass, dark coupling, kinetic-mixing portal.

history: Carlson-Machacek-Hall (1992), Spergel-Steinhardt (2000) first proposed self-interacting DM. Velocity-dependent Yukawa-mediator framework: Buckley-Fox (2010), Loeb-Weiner (2011). Comprehensive review: Tulin and Yu (2017), 'Dark matter self-interactions and small scale structure', Phys. Rep. 730, 1, arXiv:1705.02358.

references.citation: Tulin and Yu, 'Dark matter self-interactions and small scale structure', Phys. Rep. 730, 1 (2018)
Spergel and Steinhardt, 'Observational evidence for self-interacting cold dark matter', Phys. Rev. Lett. 84, 3760 (2000)
